import { useState } from "react"; import { Dialog, DialogTitle, DialogContent, DialogActions, Button, Input } from "@sistent/sistent"; export const BasicDialogDemo = () => { const [open, setOpen] = useState(false); const handleOpen = () => setOpen(true); const handleClose = () => setOpen(false); return ( <> Confirm Action Do you want to proceed with this action? ); }; export const FullScreenDialogDemo = () => { const [open, setOpen] = useState(false); const handleOpen = () => setOpen(true); const handleClose = () => setOpen(false); return ( <> Full-Screen Dialog

This dialog stretches to full screen. Use it when the user's full attention is needed for complex tasks or on mobile devices.

); }; export const FormDialogDemo = () => { const [open, setOpen] = useState(false); const handleOpen = () => setOpen(true); const handleClose = () => setOpen(false); return ( <> Subscribe
); }; The `Dialog` component is essential for creating focused user experiences that require immediate attention. It provides a controlled way to present forms, confirmations, and detailed information without navigating away from the current context.

Basic Dialog

 A simple modal dialog with title, content, and action buttons. This is the most common pattern for confirmations and simple interactions.
setOpen(true); const handleClose = () => setOpen(false); Confirm Action Do you want to proceed with this action? `} />

Full-Screen Dialog

 Full-screen dialogs are useful for complex forms or when you need the user's complete attention. They take up the entire viewport and are ideal for mobile devices.
setOpenFull(true)}> Open Full-Screen Dialog setOpenFull(false)} fullScreen maxWidth="lg" > Full-Screen Dialog

This dialog stretches to full screen. Use it when the user's full attention is needed.

`} />

Dialog with Form

 Dialogs can contain forms for collecting user input. This pattern is common for creating, editing, or subscribing workflows that don't require a full page.
setOpenForm(true)}> Open Form Dialog setOpenForm(false)}> Subscribe
`} />

frontmatter.title: Dialog Code
frontmatter.description: Dialog components provide modal windows that focus user attention on specific tasks or information. They overlay the main content and require user interaction before returning to the underlying interface.
